description: Whitelist of external commands that can be executed via 'agence /<command>'

tf-shortcut-commands._comment: Shortcuts handled directly in mode_external via route_tf_command (AIPOLICY tier model). Not subject to terraform-commands whitelist — these bypass the jq check. Listed here for documentation only.
tf-shortcut-commands._tiers: T0=auto-execute  T2=warn+confirm (infra state mutation)  T3=blocked (destroy — HIGHEST ESCALATION)
tf-shortcut-commands._policy: See codex/AIPOLICY.yaml terraform section for canonical whitelist/blacklist

tfauth.desc: terraform login — auth check / Terraform Cloud registry (whitelist)

tfdestroy.desc: terraform apply --destroy — HIGHEST ESCALATION: BLOCKED (run manually)

precommit.desc: pre-commit run [args] — git pre-commit hooks / code quality (whitelist)

gh-shortcut-commands._comment: Shortcuts handled directly in mode_external via route_gh_command (AIPOLICY tier model). All pass args intact to 'gh', including --help. Not subject to commands.json whitelist — bypass the jq check.
gh-shortcut-commands._policy: See codex/AIPOLICY.yaml github_cli section for canonical whitelist/blacklist

ghremote.desc: gh repo view — show current repo info (AIPOLICY whitelist)

ghpull.desc: gh pr list (default T0) | gh pr <subcmd> — merge/close=T2

ghlog.desc: gh run list — GitOps actions run history (AIPOLICY whitelist)

ghmerge.desc: gh pr merge — T2 confirmation required (AIPOLICY blacklist)

ghrun.desc: gh run list (default T0) | gh run <subcmd> — cancel/rerun=T2

ghflow.desc: gh workflow list (default T0) | gh workflow <subcmd> — run/enable=T2

ghissue.desc: gh issue list (default T0) | gh issue <subcmd> — close/create=T2

ghauth.desc: gh auth status — legacy handler (uses aido), kept for compat

ghlogin.desc: gh auth login — interactive login (legacy handler)

ghstatus.desc: gh repo view + gh pr list — legacy combined handler

ghcommit.desc: git commit via gh auth — legacy handler

ghpush.desc: git push via gh auth — legacy handler

git-shortcut-commands._comment: Shortcuts handled directly in mode_external via route_git_command (AIPOLICY tier model). Not subject to commands.json whitelist — these bypass the jq check. Listed here for documentation only.
git-shortcut-commands._conflicts: aider also uses /git /commit /diff in its REPL — different tool context, no runtime collision

pull.desc: git pull — merges remote state (AIPOLICY blacklist, warn+confirm)

push.desc: git push — handled by existing /push handler (warn+confirm)

commit.desc: git commit — handled by existing /commit handler (message+confirm)

notes: Commands can be invoked via: agence /<command>
Whitelist enforced for security and auditability
Add new commands by extending this file
Each command must be explicitly permitted
